title: Loersch Winery - Discovery of the year 2025/2026 – Moselle
summary: We should actually be celebrating this well-known winery as a rediscovery, as it has only recently started to regularly present its wines to us again after a break of over a decade. But time has not been wasted. Very good wines have been produced here for a long time, but they have probably never been as brilliant, exciting, and complex as they are today—from the dry wines to the stunning noble sweet wines.
content: We should actually be celebrating this well-known winery as a rediscovery, as it has only recently started to regularly present its wines to us again after a break of over a decade. But time has not been wasted. Very good wines have been produced here for a long time, but they have probably never been as brilliant, exciting, and complex as they are today—from the dry wines to the stunning noble sweet wines.

title: Max Ferd. Richter Winery - Collection of the year 2025/2026 – Mosel
summary: Since Constantin Richter took over his parents' winery, it has moved ever closer to the top of the Mosel, Saar, and Ruwer wine estates. Today, his classic off-dry and sweet Rieslings are on a par with those of far more famous wineries. However, Richter has almost no competition in one area: he produces bone-dry, top-quality Rieslings from some of the legendary vineyards of the Middle Moselle, the likes of which have probably never been seen here before.
content: Since Constantin Richter took over his parents' winery, it has moved ever closer to the top of the Mosel, Saar, and Ruwer wine estates. Today, his classic off-dry and sweet Rieslings are on a par with those of far more famous wineries. However, Richter has almost no competition in one area: he produces bone-dry, top-quality Rieslings from some of the legendary vineyards of the Middle Moselle, the likes of which have probably never been seen here before.

title: Schloss Schönberg Winery - Discovery of the year 2025/2026 – Hessische Bergstrasse
summary: Schloss Schönberg is the sister company of Sekthaus Griesel, which has earned itself a firm place among the best sparkling wine producers in Germany in recent years. So it is no wonder that the still wines are also of remarkable quality. And that is almost an understatement; we have never tasted such good wines from the Hessische Bergstrasse!
content: Schloss Schönberg is the sister company of Sekthaus Griesel, which has earned itself a firm place among the best sparkling wine producers in Germany in recent years. So it is no wonder that the still wines are also of remarkable quality. And that is almost an understatement; we have never tasted such good wines from the Hessische Bergstrasse!

title: Ilmbacher Hof Winery - Discovery of the year 2025/2026 – Franconia
summary: This small winery is somewhat overshadowed by far more famous wineries in Iphofen. Wrongly so: here you will not only find excellent Silvaner (also as sparkling wine), but also one of the best Müller-Thurgau wines ever.
content: This small winery is somewhat overshadowed by far more famous wineries in Iphofen. Wrongly so: here you will not only find excellent Silvaner (also as sparkling wine), but also one of the best Müller-Thurgau wines ever.

title: Richard Östreicher Winery - Collection of the year 2025/2026 – Franconia
summary: Richard Östreicher's cellar now produces some of the finest white wines in Franconia, indeed in the whole of Germany. We have not yet experienced better Silvaner here, and the Chardonnay is also of great class. The wines combine concentration, depth, and expressiveness with finesse and inner calm in a way that few producers can achieve. And by the way, one of the best red Bordeaux blends in the country is also produced here.
content: Richard Östreicher's cellar now produces some of the finest white wines in Franconia, indeed in the whole of Germany. We have not yet experienced better Silvaner here, and the Chardonnay is also of great class. The wines combine concentration, depth, and expressiveness with finesse and inner calm in a way that few producers can achieve. And by the way, one of the best red Bordeaux blends in the country is also produced here.

title: Rebholz Winery - Discovery of the year 2025/2026 – Baden
summary: For the first time, we tasted a small series of wines from the Lake Constance winery Rebholz, all of which we liked in their medium-bodied, sometimes mellow, and generally warm style. Some of the samples showed a certain class. This is a winery to keep an eye on.
content: For the first time, we tasted a small series of wines from the Lake Constance winery Rebholz, all of which we liked in their medium-bodied, sometimes mellow, and generally warm style. Some of the samples showed a certain class. This is a winery to keep an eye on.

title: Gebrüder Mathis Winery - Collection of the year 2025/2026 – Baden
summary: Under the name Kalkbödele, the winery has long been known for good, often very good wines from the Tuniberg. Under winemaker Severin Stich and cellar master Sabeth Sedlatschek, the quality has once again increased significantly: The wines have become finer, nobler, and more precise across the board and are even among the best of their kind in the country. The Tuniberg has long been overshadowed by the neighbouring Kaiserstuhl. The new generation of winemakers at the Mathis brothers' winery is clearly working on changing this.
content: Under the name Kalkbödele, the winery has long been known for good, often very good wines from the Tuniberg. Under winemaker Severin Stich and cellar master Sabeth Sedlatschek, the quality has once again increased significantly: The wines have become finer, nobler, and more precise across the board and are even among the best of their kind in the country. The Tuniberg has long been overshadowed by the neighbouring Kaiserstuhl. The new generation of winemakers at the Mathis brothers' winery is clearly working on changing this.

title: Winery J.J. Adeneuer - Collection of the year 2025/2026 – Ahr
summary: This is not the first time we have honoured the Adeneuer family with the Collection of the Year. What impresses us here is not only the sheer quality, but also the very individual character of the wines. They are not copies of great role models, but rather first-class images of their origins. Even the inexpensive entry-level wines are of remarkable class.
content: This is not the first time we have honoured the Adeneuer family with the Collection of the Year. What impresses us here is not only the sheer quality, but also the very individual character of the wines. They are not copies of great role models, but rather first-class images of their origins. Even the inexpensive entry-level wines are of remarkable class.

title: The Collections and Discoveries of the Year - Tasting season 2025/2026
summary: Marcus Hofschuster and Kim Schreiber have honoured 31 wineries from four countries this year. 18 received the prize for the "Collection of the Year", 13 wineries are their "Discoveries of the Year". Here are the winners of the season!
content: Marcus Hofschuster and Kim Schreiber have honoured 31 wineries from four countries this year. 18 received the prize for the "Collection of the Year", 13 wineries are their "Discoveries of the Year". Here are the winners of the season!

title: The northernmost Grüner Veltliner in the world comes from Finland - Exclusive tasting: Wine from the sauna
summary: In Finland, Grüner Veltliner from the northernmost origin in the world was harvested for the first time in 2025. wein.plus editor Alexander Lupersböck was the first foreign journalist to taste it exclusively.
content: In Finland, Grüner Veltliner from the northernmost origin in the world was harvested for the first time in 2025. wein.plus editor Alexander Lupersböck was the first foreign journalist to taste it exclusively.

title: Rebels among themselves - Tasting: Landweinmarkt in Eimeldingen
summary: Regional wine is the second lowest level in the European wine classification. But that shouldn't worry wine fans. Because many of the wines labelled as regional wine have real class. At the "Badischer Landweinmarkt" in Eimeldingen on Friday, 24 April, everyone can see this for themselves. We've already had a taste.
content: Regional wine is the second lowest level in the European wine classification. But that shouldn't worry wine fans. Because many of the wines labelled as regional wine have real class. At the "Badischer Landweinmarkt" in Eimeldingen on Friday, 24 April, everyone can see this for themselves. We've already had a taste.

title: "I like wines that I don't immediately fall in love with" - Interview: Ilija Trojanow
summary: The author Ilija Trojanow talks about his fascination with wine, the importance of inebriation, the connection between winemakers and writers—and why wine is only good when it makes a conversation possible.
content: The author Ilija Trojanow talks about his fascination with wine, the importance of inebriation, the connection between winemakers and writers—and why wine is only good when it makes a conversation possible.

title: Better than ever - Tasting: Pinot varieties
summary: With over 1,700 wines currently being tasted, it is clear that the variety and quality of white and red wines from Pinot varieties has probably never been as high as it is today. Even difficult vintages do little to change this.
content: With over 1,700 wines currently being tasted, it is clear that the variety and quality of white and red wines from Pinot varieties has probably never been as high as it is today. Even difficult vintages do little to change this.

title: Wine and catering tents from Pro-Tent - Advertisement
summary: Whether wine tasting as a special event, a canopy for the outdoor area of a wine tavern or a prestigious stand at a wine festival—winegrowers and winemakers rely on Pro-Tent's wine and catering tents. Especially for temporary stands, they benefit from the quick assembly and dismantling and the convenient transport system.
content: Whether wine tasting as a special event, a canopy for the outdoor area of a wine tavern or a prestigious stand at a wine festival—winegrowers and winemakers rely on Pro-Tent's wine and catering tents. Especially for temporary stands, they benefit from the quick assembly and dismantling and the convenient transport system.

title: These wines make you happy! - The half-year winners in all categories
summary: In view of the news situation, the only thing that helps is to have a good time. Perhaps with a great Riesling, Burgundy, or sparkling wine? With our selection of the best wines from the past few months, the sun will shine even on hard days. We promise!
content: In view of the news situation, the only thing that helps is to have a good time. Perhaps with a great Riesling, Burgundy, or sparkling wine? With our selection of the best wines from the past few months, the sun will shine even on hard days. We promise!

title: "There are no corked bottles hanging on vines" - Interview: Florian Weingart
summary: Winemaker Florian Weingart from Spay (Mittelrhein) criticises the new German classification system, which is based on the VDP system. In this interview, he explains why he considers the new rules to be technically incorrect and not equal for all wineries.
content: Winemaker Florian Weingart from Spay (Mittelrhein) criticises the new German classification system, which is based on the VDP system. In this interview, he explains why he considers the new rules to be technically incorrect and not equal for all wineries.

title: Our price enjoyment winners of the past months - Tasting: The best wines for little money
summary: How big is your budget for a bottle of wine? A maximum of ten euros, up to 15 or 20 euros? We make it easy for you and present our favourites for spring. In all three price categories, in red, rosé and white, dry and sweet, plus sparkling wines up to 25 euros.
content: How big is your budget for a bottle of wine? A maximum of ten euros, up to 15 or 20 euros? We make it easy for you and present our favourites for spring. In all three price categories, in red, rosé and white, dry and sweet, plus sparkling wines up to 25 euros.

title: Everything is real—except the wine - Recognising counterfeit wine
summary: There is a kind of arms race between fraudsters and traders: even professionals find it difficult to detect counterfeit wines today. But they are fighting back with high-tech tools and new methods of detection.
content: There is a kind of arms race between fraudsters and traders: even professionals find it difficult to detect counterfeit wines today. But they are fighting back with high-tech tools and new methods of detection.

title: Golden Grape Awards: These are the winners 2025/2026 - Award ceremony at the Eurovino in Karlsruhe
summary: In the GOLDEN GRAPE AWARDS competitions for Silvaner, Sauvignon Blanc, Riesling, Pinot varieties, and Blaufränkisch, we tasted over 4,400 wines from 994 wineries. Here are the winners! #goldengrapeawards #weinplus

title: A tough year with a good ending - Tasting: Riesling 2024
summary: First came the frost, then the rain—not an easy year for German winegrowers. But what they made of it is amazing: the Riesling wines are often excellent, as Marcus Hofschuster and Kim Schreiber discovered during their big tasting.
content: First came the frost, then the rain—not an easy year for German winegrowers. But what they made of it is amazing: the Riesling wines are often excellent, as Marcus Hofschuster and Kim Schreiber discovered during their big tasting.
